behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted file paths, command names (list, close-all, kill-all), flag (-b), category (browsers), strings (Browser ', [open], [closed]) are named explicitly in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the desired behavior and naming but does not provide code-level edits or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_b_flag_alias extracts via regex /if\s*\(\s*args\.b\s*\)\s*\{([\s\S]*?)\}/ and requires deleting args.b; a valid implementation using yargs alias config or 'args.session = args.b' without a wrapping `if (args.b) {...}` block would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Node scripts and even eval extracted code (e.g., new Function to run the -b normalization block) rather than only doing string checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require both presence of new names and absence of old names, plus executing extracted logic — a stub cannot satisfy them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Renamed commands, -b alias, browsers category, Browser terminology, [open]/[closed] markers, and doc updates are each tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass pr_diff, agent_config) and named descriptively.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and command names in instruction.md are consistent with tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to look at (appropriate for the task scope) without pointing at specific lines or the exact code change.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is primarily a code change task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
